# Coding Agent Feature Matrix
> Compare 17 coding agents, CLIs, and IDEs across features.
> https://compare.ainorthstar.tech | Updated 2026-05-25

Agent             Rel    FF     Rul   Skl   Hks   MCP   Cmd   Sub   Mod   App   San   Res   Con   Hdl   Fmt   Sta   Tel   Hst  BYOM
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Aider             Jun '2 CLI        ~     ~     ~     N     N     ~     Y     ~     N     N     ~     Y     N     N     Y     N     Y
Amp               May '2 CLI/Ext    Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ~     ~     N     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     ?     N     ~
Antigravity CLI   May '2 CLI        Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     ?     N     ~     ~     Y     Y     Y     ?     ?     ?     N     N
Antigravity IDE   2025  IDE        ~     ~     ?     ~     ~     ?     ?     ?     ?     Y     Y     N     ?     ?     ?     N     ?
Claude Code       Feb '2 CLI/IDE    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     ~
Cline             Jul '2 CLI/IDE    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     ~     Y     Y     N     Y     N     Y
OpenAI Codex CLI  Apr '2 CLI/Ext    Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y
Cursor            2023  CLI/IDE    Y     Y     ?     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     ~     Y     Y     Y
Devin             Dec '2 CLI/Ext    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ?     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     N     ?     Y     ?
Gemini CLI        Jun '2 CLI        Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     ?     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     ?     Y     N     ~
GitHub Copilot    Jun '2 CLI/IDE    Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     ~     ~     Y     Y     Y     ~     N     ~     Y     Y
Kilo Code         Feb '2 CLI/IDE    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ~     Y
Kimi CLI          Oct '2 CLI/Ext    Y     Y     ~     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ?     N     Y
OpenCode          2025  CLI/SDK    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     ?     Y     N     Y
Qwen Code         Jun '2 CLI/Ext    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y
Roo Code          Mid-20 CLI/IDE    Y     Y     N     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     ~     Y     Y     ~     N     Y     N     Y
Windsurf Cascade  Nov '2 CLI/IDE    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     ~     ~     ~     ~     Y     ~

## Feature legend
Code = Y: full | ~: partial | N: none | ?: unknown |  : 
FF = Form factor (CLI/IDE/Ext/SDK/Web)

## Agent details

### Aider
- Docs: https://aider.chat/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/Aider-AI/aider
- Website: https://aider.chat/
- Form factor: CLI
- Released: Jun '23
- Latest update: Aug '25
- Rules: partial — Supports CONVENTIONS.md loaded via --read or .aider.conf.yml. No equivalent of a persistent auto-loaded CLAUDE.md file.
- Skills: partial — Has /save and /load commands to persist/replay sequences of slash commands. Not a full reusable skill system like Cursor skills.
- Hooks: partial — Only git pre-commit hook bypass via --git-commit-verify. No lifecycle hooks (session-start, session-end, etc.).
- Mcp Servers: none — No MCP (Model Context Protocol) support detected in documentation, codebase, or feature listings.
- Custom Commands: none — 40+ built-in slash commands but no mechanism for users to define custom slash commands.
- Subagents: partial — Architect/Editor mode uses two models but no general subagent delegation system. No spawning of independent worker agents.
- Model Selection: full — Full model selection via --model, --weak-model, --editor-model. In-chat /model command. Model aliases supported.
- Approval Mode: partial — Has --yes-always to auto-accept all confirmations. No granular approval mode like Cursor's always/accept-tool/plan-then-act.
- Sandbox Mode: none — No filesystem or network sandbox. Has --dry-run flag that prevents file modification. Aider edits files directly in the working git repo.
- Resume: none — No --resume flag. Has --restore-chat-history which restores previous chat messages but does not resume session state.
- Continue: partial — No --continue flag for auto-resuming the latest session. --restore-chat-history restores message history but is manual.
- Non Interactive: full — Full non-interactive mode via --message/-m (send instruction, edit files, exit). Also --apply, --commit, --lint, --test.
- Output Format: none — No structured machine-readable output format (JSON, etc.). Has --no-pretty for plain text, --verbose for debug info.
- Statusline: none — No configurable status line. Has extensive color customization but no status bar/line concept.
- Telemetry: full — Opt-in anonymous analytics via PostHog. Permanently disable with --analytics-disable. Never collects code/chat/keys.
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs entirely locally via pip. No hosted agent platform.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Connects to any LLM via litellm. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, OpenRouter, any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and more.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0)
- Notes: Aider is a mature open-source CLI coding agent (45k+ stars) since June 2023. Deep git integration, repo mapping for codebase context, extensive LLM provider support through litellm. No published embed SDK (CLI/scripting only). Notable gaps vs newer agents: no MCP, no user-defined slash commands, no sandbox, no subagent delegation, no resume/continue flags, no structured output. Apache-2.0 licensed.

### Amp
- Docs: https://ampcode.com/manual
- Website: https://ampcode.app/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: May '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Full support via AGENTS.md files (plural). Scanned from cwd, parent dirs, subtrees, $HOME/.config/amp/, /etc/ampcode/. Supports @-mentions, globs, YAML frontmatter.
- Skills: full — Full support. Skills are directories with SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter. Locations: .agents/skills/ (project), ~/.config/agents/skills/ (user). Can bundle MCP servers via mcp.json.
- Hooks: full — Full support via Plugin API (TypeScript). Events: session.start, agent.start, tool.call, tool.result, agent.end. Plugins can respond with allow/reject/modify/synthesize actions.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full support. Add via CLI (amp mcp add) or config. Local (command/args/env) and remote (url/headers) servers. OAuth auto-flow. Skill bundling for lazy loading.
- Custom Commands: none — Removed January 29, 2026. Replaced by skills and command palette (Ctrl+O) instead of slash commands.
- Subagents: full — Full support. Spawned via Task tool. Built-in specialized subagents: Oracle (GPT-5.4), Librarian (code search), Painter (image gen), Review, Search.
- Model Selection: partial — Pre-set modes (smart=Claude Opus 4.7, deep=GPT-5.5 reasoning, rush=GPT-5.5 no-reasoning, free=mixed). You choose the mode, not the exact model.
- Approval Mode: partial — Default: no approval (dangerously-allow-all is now default). Custom approval policies possible via Plugin API (tool.call event).
- Sandbox Mode: none — No built-in sandbox. Documentation suggests external isolated dev environments for untrusted code.
- Resume: full — Full support. 'amp threads continue [thread id]' documented. Thread referencing (@T-xxx or URL) across sessions.
- Continue: full — Full support. /continue command in CLI to pick old threads. Threads persist at ampcode.com/threads.
- Non Interactive: full — Full support. amp --execute / amp -x for headless mode. Set AMP_API_KEY env var for CI/CD. Auto-detects piped stdout.
- Output Format: full — Full support. --stream-json flag with --execute for Claude Code-compatible NDJSON streaming output. Multiple schema types.
- Statusline: none — Not supported. Custom themes removed in the May 6, 2026 rebuild. No statusline customization.
- Telemetry: unknown — No explicit telemetry settings found in documentation. No on/off telemetry toggle mentioned in manual or settings.
- Hosted Agent: none — CLI runs locally. Threads shared via web but no managed agent platform.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — No BYOM for the main agent (picks models per mode from providers). MCP servers can connect to external tools, and Plugin API allows custom tool registration.
- Pricing: Usage-based with free tier
- Notes: Amp is opinionated and aggressively removes features it considers obsolete (custom commands, slash commands, themes). Focuses on frontier models. Plugin API provides extensive extensibility for those who need it. Closed-source product; some auxiliary tools open source on GitHub.

### Antigravity CLI
- Docs: https://antigravity.google/docs/cli-features
- Website: https://antigravity.google/
- Form factor: CLI
- Released: May '26
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Plugin rules live under plugin rules directories.
- Skills: full — Plugin skills are supported.
- Hooks: full — Plugin hooks are supported.
- Mcp Servers: full — Plugin mcp_config.json is supported.
- Custom Commands: partial — Implemented through plugin-provided capabilities rather than a classic slash-command folder.
- Subagents: unknown
- Model Selection: none — agy --help does not expose a model flag as of this dataset.
- Approval Mode: partial — Supports --dangerously-skip-permissions.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Supports --sandbox for terminal restrictions.
- Resume: full — Supports --continue and --conversation.
- Continue: full — Supports --continue for latest conversation and --conversation for a specific conversation.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports --print / --prompt.
- Output Format: unknown
- Statusline: unknown
- Telemetry: unknown
- Hosted Agent: none — Local CLI tool.
- Custom Model Provider: none — No arbitrary provider configuration found in public CLI help/docs.
- Pricing: Free (generous weekly rate limits), Google AI Pro/Ultra (undisclosed), Organization (consumption-based via Google Cloud)
- Notes: Distinct from Gemini CLI and the older Antigravity IDE. No published programmatic SDK found.

### Antigravity IDE
- Docs: https://antigravity.google/docs/
- Website: https://antigravity.google/
- Form factor: IDE
- Released: 2025
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: partial — IDE has persistent agent context, but not the same plugin rules path as Antigravity CLI.
- Skills: partial — Local IDE data includes skills, but public docs are less explicit than CLI plugin docs.
- Hooks: unknown
- Mcp Servers: partial — Local IDE data includes mcp_config.json, but CLI plugin MCP is documented more clearly.
- Custom Commands: partial — Global workflows are present in local IDE data.
- Subagents: unknown
- Model Selection: unknown
- Approval Mode: unknown
- Sandbox Mode: unknown
- Resume: full — IDE stores conversation/brain state.
- Continue: full — IDE conversation state can be continued from existing history.
- Non Interactive: none — Use Antigravity CLI for headless use.
- Output Format: unknown
- Statusline: unknown
- Telemetry: unknown
- Hosted Agent: none — Local IDE surface.
- Custom Model Provider: unknown
- Pricing: Free (generous weekly rate limits), Google AI Pro/Ultra (undisclosed), Organization (consumption-based via Google Cloud)
- Notes: Included as a distinct IDE surface because it is not the same as the new agy CLI. No published programmatic SDK found.

### Claude Code
- Docs: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview
- GitHub: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code
- Website: https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension, SDK
- Released: Feb '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Uses CLAUDE.md memory files.
- Skills: full — Supports skills.
- Hooks: full — Supports lifecycle hooks.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers.
- Custom Commands: full — Supports slash commands.
- Subagents: full — Supports custom subagents.
- Model Selection: full — Model can be configured.
- Approval Mode: full — Permission mode is configurable.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Permissions and allowed tools provide sandbox-like controls.
- Resume: full — Supports --resume.
- Continue: full — Supports --continue for the most recent conversation.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports --print.
- Output Format: full — Supports output format controls.
- Statusline: full — Supports custom status line.
- Telemetry: full — Settings include telemetry and privacy controls.
- Hosted Agent: partial — CLI runs locally; managed/cloud-based session execution is not directly exposed as a distinct platform.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — Supports Bedrock/Vertex and related integrations, not arbitrary providers in the same way as OpenAI-compatible CLIs.
- Pricing: Included in Pro $20/mo, Max 5x $100/mo, Max 20x $200/mo, Team $25-125/seat/mo, Enterprise $20/seat + API rates

### Cline
- Docs: https://docs.cline.bot/
- GitHub: https://github.com/cline/cline
- Website: https://cline.bot/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension, SDK
- Released: Jul '24
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Rich rule system: .clinerules/ directory, global ~/Documents/Cline/Rules/, conditional rules with YAML frontmatter globs, auto-detection of .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, AGENTS.md.
- Skills: full — Full skill system: SKILL.md files in .cline/skills/ or ~/.cline/skills/, YAML frontmatter, progressive loading, slash command triggering, skill toggles.
- Hooks: full — Lifecycle hooks via SDK plugin system: 18 hook stages (input, runtime_event, session_start, turn_start, tool_call_before/after, etc.). Also ~/.cline/hooks/ directory.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP support with STDIO and HTTP/SSE transports, MCP Marketplace for one-click install, cline mcp CLI wizard.
- Custom Commands: full — Built-in slash commands plus skills auto-register as slash commands. SDK plugins can also register custom commands.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent system: use_subagents tool spawns parallel read-only research agents with separate context/token budgets.
- Model Selection: full — 30+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP Vertex, OpenRouter (200+ models), Ollama, LM Studio, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- Approval Mode: full — Granular auto-approve per category: Read, Edit, Execute, Browser, MCP. YOLO mode for everything. --auto-approve flag.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Sandbox mode via CLINE_SANDBOX env var and CLINE_SANDBOX_DATA_DIR for isolated session storage. No containerization.
- Resume: full — Full session resume via --id flag. cline history lists all sessions. Checkpoints enable rollback.
- Continue: partial — --continue flag exists but is tied to --worktree. No standalone 'continue latest' flag. Pipe-chaining supported.
- Non Interactive: full — Full headless mode. Auto-triggered with --json flag, piped stdin, or redirected stdout. Works in CI/CD.
- Output Format: full — Full JSON output via --json flag. Newline-delimited JSON objects with type, text, ts, say/ask subtype, reasoning, partial.
- Statusline: none — No customizable status line found in documentation or CLI reference.
- Telemetry: full — Anonymous telemetry with user toggle. Never captures code/file contents. Enterprise: OpenTelemetry integration (OTLP export to Datadog/Grafana/Splunk).
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint supported. 30+ documented providers. BYOK model.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0), Enterprise custom pricing
- Notes: Cline has the most comprehensive feature set of any coding agent in this matrix — 17/20 fields at full support. Open source (Apache 2.0), originally 'Claude Dev'. Started as VS Code extension, expanded to CLI and SDK. 8M+ installs.

### OpenAI Codex CLI
- Docs: https://developers.openai.com/codex/
- GitHub: https://github.com/openai/codex
- Website: https://openai.com/codex/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: Apr '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Uses AGENTS.md instructions.
- Skills: full — Supports SKILL.md-based skills.
- Hooks: full — Supports Codex hooks.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers.
- Custom Commands: partial — CLI supports slash-like workflows through prompts and tooling, but not a first-class command folder in this matrix.
- Subagents: full — Codex supports sub-agent/delegation workflows in agent contexts.
- Model Selection: full — CLI accepts model configuration/flags.
- Approval Mode: full — Approval policy can be configured.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Filesystem sandbox modes can be configured.
- Resume: full — Supports resume.
- Continue: full — Supports continuing a previous session through resume/latest-session workflows.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports exec/headless use.
- Output Format: full — CLI supports structured output modes.
- Statusline: full — Supports statusline configuration in local CLI settings.
- Telemetry: full — Telemetry settings are documented in the CLI repository.
- Hosted Agent: full — Can run locally or on platform.openai.com managed infrastructure.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Supports custom model providers in config.
- Pricing: Free (limited), Plus $20/mo, Pro $100-200/mo, Business pay-as-you-go, API usage-based

### Cursor
- Docs: https://docs.cursor.com/
- Website: https://cursor.com/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension, SDK
- Released: 2023
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Supports project and user rules.
- Skills: full — Supports skills.
- Hooks: unknown — No lifecycle hooks documented for IDE or CLI.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers in both IDE and CLI.
- Custom Commands: full — CLI exposes full command controls; IDE has reusable rule/workflow patterns.
- Subagents: partial — Agent tasks are supported; first-class named subagents not modeled like Claude Code.
- Model Selection: full — Supports model settings in IDE and CLI.
- Approval Mode: full — Agent modes and CLI yolo/force controls expose approval controls.
- Sandbox Mode: full — CLI exposes sandbox controls; IDE trust and agent controls provide partial sandboxing.
- Resume: full — Composer/chat history in IDE and CLI resume/continue workflows support session resumption.
- Continue: full — Supports --continue in CLI and conversation history in IDE.
- Non Interactive: full — CLI supports print/headless mode; IDE is interactive only.
- Output Format: partial — CLI has partial structured output support; IDE has none.
- Statusline: partial — Editor status bar customization inherited from VS Code, not an agent-specific statusline.
- Telemetry: full — Cursor documents privacy settings.
- Hosted Agent: full — CLI agent can execute in Cursor's cloud runtime.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Supports custom API keys/models in IDE and CLI.
- Pricing: Pro $20/mo, Business $40/user/mo
- Notes: Cursor is a VS Code-derived AI IDE with a companion CLI (cursor-agent) for headless/CI use and @cursor/sdk for programmatic local/cloud agents.

### Devin
- Docs: https://docs.devin.ai/
- Website: https://devin.ai/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK, Web
- Released: Dec '24
- Latest update: Feb '26
- Rules: full — AGENTS.md files in repos, Knowledge (persistent org-level instructions with trigger descriptions), Skills & Rules settings.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills standard support. SKILL.md files in .agents/skills/. Supports YAML frontmatter, dynamic $ARGUMENTS and !command blocks. Also Playbooks system.
- Hooks: full — Automations engine: Slack/GitHub/Linear/schedule/cron/webhook triggers. Actions include start session, message session, triage. Payload filtering and secret auth.
- Mcp Servers: full — MCP Marketplace with 40+ pre-built integrations. Supports STDIO, SSE, and HTTP transports. Custom MCP server tools for session/playbook/knowledge management.
- Custom Commands: full — Built-in slash commands (/plan, /review, /test, /think-hard, /implement) plus org-level custom slash commands. Also @skills:name syntax and macros.
- Subagents: full — Yes, called 'Managed Devins'. Coordinator spawns child Devin sessions in isolated VMs for parallel work. Auto-triage also spawns sub-devins.
- Model Selection: unknown — Not explicitly user-facing. Devin offers 'Fast Mode' (~2x faster at 4x ACU). No documentation about letting users choose the underlying LLM model.
- Approval Mode: partial — 'Secure Mode' (disables native deployment, restricts outbound access). ACU caps, RBAC, PR review approval. No granular approval per action.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Full isolation. Every session gets a fresh ephemeral snapshot. Enterprise VPC deployment option. VPN configuration supported.
- Resume: full — API auto-resumes suspended sessions. CLI /resume alias. PR Resuming feature (Apr 2026). Archive/unarchive with undo.
- Continue: full — CLI /handoff transfers to cloud Devin with full context (git branch + conversation history). API session messaging for continuation.
- Non Interactive: full — Full headless/API mode. REST API with service user auth. Automations run fully autonomously (CI fixers, scheduled sessions, auto-triage).
- Output Format: partial — API returns structured JSON. Sessions support structured output schemas. No documented machine-readable mode for the CLI itself.
- Statusline: none — No statusline feature found in any documentation. Web app shows session status indicators but no CLI statusline.
- Telemetry: unknown — No user-configurable telemetry setting found in documentation. Enterprise audit logs available. Privacy controls mentioned but no opt-in/opt-out toggle.
- Hosted Agent: full — Primarily cloud-hosted at app.devin.ai; Devin CLI bridges to cloud agent.
- Custom Model Provider: unknown — Cerebras mentioned as enterprise-ready inference provider. No self-serve BYO model option documented.
- Pricing: Free, Pro $20/mo, Max $200/mo, Teams $80+/mo
- Notes: Devin pioneered the autonomous AI software engineer category. Focuses on enterprise: VM isolation, managed sub-agents, automations. Higher-end pricing reflects the hosted, managed approach. Windsurf acquisition announced April 2026.

### Gemini CLI
- Docs: https://google-gemini.github.io/gemini-cli/
- GitHub: https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli
- Website: https://blog.google/technology/developers/introducing-gemini-cli-open-source-ai-agent/
- Form factor: CLI
- Status: Deprecated
- Released: Jun '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Uses GEMINI.md context files.
- Skills: partial — Extensible through tools/extensions rather than the same SKILL.md contract.
- Hooks: full — Supports hooks in settings.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers.
- Custom Commands: full — Supports commands.
- Subagents: unknown
- Model Selection: full — Model can be configured.
- Approval Mode: full — Approval mode is configurable.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Sandbox settings are documented.
- Resume: full — Supports resume workflows.
- Continue: full — Supports continuing a previous session through resume/latest-session workflows.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports prompt/headless use.
- Output Format: partial
- Statusline: unknown
- Telemetry: full — Telemetry is documented.
- Hosted Agent: none — Local CLI tool backed by Google Gemini API.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — Primarily Gemini/Google-oriented provider configuration.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0), Gemini API usage billed separately
- Notes: Deprecated: Google points new CLI work to Antigravity CLI. Kept as an explicit agent-launch option, but excluded from random selection in this repo. Headless/scripting mode only; no published agent embed SDK.

### GitHub Copilot
- Docs: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/coding-agent/coding-agent
- GitHub: https://github.com/features/copilot
- Website: https://github.com/features/copilot
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension, SDK
- Released: Jun '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Multiple layers: .github/copilot-instructions.md (repo-wide), path-specific .github/instructions/**, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, personal instructions, org-level.
- Skills: full — Open Agent Skills standard. .github/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills. Personal skills in ~/.copilot/skills. gh skill CLI for discovery/install.
- Hooks: full — Full lifecycle hook system: sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse (approve/deny), postToolUse, agentStop, subagentStop, errorOccurred. .github/hooks/*.json.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP in cloud agent, CLI, and IDE. Supports local, stdio, http, sse transports. Built-in GitHub MCP server. Enterprise allowlist controls.
- Custom Commands: partial — Built-in slash commands in IDE (/fix, /tests, /explain, /help) and CLI (/delegate, /pr, /resume). Prompt files (*.prompt.md) for reusable snippets. No user-defined custom slash commands.
- Subagents: full — Subagents in VS Code and JetBrains IDE agent mode. Isolated context window. Invocation: automatic, direct, or via #runSubagent. Cannot create sub-subagents.
- Model Selection: full — Models from Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, xAI. Auto model selection available. Each model has a premium request multiplier.
- Approval Mode: partial — Desktop app has Interactive, Plan, and Autopilot modes. preToolUse hook can approve/deny. No universal 'always ask before X' toggle.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Cloud agent runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment (natural sandbox). CLI has tool permission controls. No explicit sandbox toggle.
- Resume: full — CLI: copilot --resume opens session picker, copilot --resume SESSION-ID jumps to specific session. /resume during interactive session.
- Continue: full — CLI: copilot --continue resumes most recent session directly. IDE agent sessions persistent. Desktop app sessions in sidebar.
- Non Interactive: full — CLI programmatic/scripted usage in GitHub Actions. --non-interactive mode. Copilot SDK for API-based integration.
- Output Format: partial — Copilot SDK provides programmatic access with structured responses. No documented --json flag for general CLI output.
- Statusline: none — No statusline feature documented anywhere in GitHub Copilot's documentation.
- Telemetry: partial — Enterprise audit logging, agent session monitoring. Data retention: prompts retained 28 days. Users can opt out of training data. No granular telemetry UI.
- Hosted Agent: full — Copilot Cloud Agent runs autonomously in GitHub Actions-powered ephemeral environment.
- Custom Model Provider: full — BYOK in CLI: env vars for provider type, base URL, API key. OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, local models via Ollama.
- Pricing: Free $0, Pro $10/mo, Pro+ $39/mo, Business $19/user/mo, Enterprise $39/user/mo
- Notes: GitHub Copilot's agent capabilities span IDE, cloud, and CLI. The cloud agent is unique: runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment. Desktop app (preview) adds parallel agent sessions. Ecosystem is rapidly evolving with weekly feature drops.

### Kilo Code
- Docs: https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started
- GitHub: https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode
- Website: https://kilo.ai/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension, SDK
- Released: Feb '26
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Full support: Project Rules (via instructions key in kilo.jsonc), Global Rules (same in ~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc), AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills standard. SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Global ~/.kilo/skills/, project .kilo/skills/. Compat with .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/.
- Hooks: full — Full support via Plugin system. Lifecycle hooks: config, event (bus events), tool hooks (execute.before/after), chat hooks, permission hooks, provider hooks. 17+ hook types.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP support. Local (STDIO) and remote (HTTP Streamable) transports. OAuth 2.0. Tool permissions via allow/ask/deny. Built-in Playwright MCP.
- Custom Commands: full — Full support via Workflows (slash commands). Markdown files in ~/.config/kilo/commands/ or .kilo/commands/. YAML frontmatter with description, agent, model.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent support. Custom modes/agents with mode property: primary, subagent, or all. Per-agent prompts, models, permissions, temperature. task tool spawns sub-agents.
- Model Selection: full — 500+ models via 15+ providers. Switch mid-session with /models. Per-agent model pinning. CLI flags: -m, --model. Auto model routing.
- Approval Mode: full — Three-tier: allow (auto-approve), ask (prompt), deny (block). Glob patterns. --auto flag for autonomous mode. Per-agent permission overrides.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — No dedicated Docker/sandbox isolation. External_directory permissions control reads/writes outside workspace. Permission system with allow/deny/ask.
- Resume: full — Session continuation via --continue/-c flag. -s, --session <id> for specific sessions. --fork to fork existing sessions. /sessions slash command.
- Continue: full — --continue/-c flag on kilo, kilo run, and kilo attach commands. /sessions slash command also accepts /continue alias.
- Non Interactive: full — Multiple non-interactive modes: kilo run <message> (one-shot), --auto flag (fully autonomous), --json (JSON output), --json-io (bidirectional JSON). kilo serve for headless server.
- Output Format: full — --format flag with choices default (TUI) and json (raw JSON events). --json flag implies auto mode. --json-io for bidirectional JSON. Session export/import.
- Statusline: none — No customizable statusline feature found. Has /status slash command and /themes for theming, but no persistent status line customization.
- Telemetry: full — Enabled by default. Disable via experimental.openTelemetry: false. OpenTelemetry export when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set.
- Hosted Agent: partial — Cloud Agents feature allows web-based sessions; primary use is local via extension/CLI.
- Custom Model Provider: full — BYOK for 15+ providers. Plugin system for entirely custom providers. Local models supported. 500+ models via Kilo Gateway at zero markup.
- Pricing: Free (MIT), Kilo Pass $19-199/mo, Teams $15/user/mo
- Notes: Kilo Code is a rapidly evolving open-source coding agent (MIT, 19k+ stars). Originally forked from Continue, now independent. Notable for extensive plugin system, 500+ model support through Kilo Gateway, and cross-surface session sync.

### Kimi CLI
- Docs: https://www.kimi.com/code/docs/en/kimi-code-cli/getting-started.html
- GitHub: https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-cli
- Website: https://kimi.moonshot.cn/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: Oct '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Full AGENTS.md support. Hierarchical loading from git root to working dir, including .kimi/AGENTS.md at each level (32 KiB budget). /init command auto-generates AGENTS.md.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills format (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter). Four-tier auto-discovery. Cross-compatible with Claude/Codex skill dirs. Flow skills with Mermaid/D2 flowcharts.
- Hooks: partial — Supported (Beta). 13 lifecycle events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd, SubagentStart/Stop, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification. Shell commands via stdin.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP support. Transport: streamable HTTP, stdio, OAuth. kimi mcp add/list/test/remove/auth commands. Config in ~/.kimi/mcp.json.
- Custom Commands: partial — Custom plugins (Beta) via plugin.json with executable scripts. Slash commands (/skill:, /flow:) serve as custom invocation. No arbitrary custom aliases in config.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent support. Built-in types: coder, explore, plan. YAML agent files with inheritance. Agent tool with subagent_type, model, resume, run_in_background params.
- Model Selection: full — Full model selection. Provider types: kimi, openai_legacy, openai_responses, anthropic, gemini, vertexai. /model interactive picker. CLI flags: --model, --thinking.
- Approval Mode: full — Full system: Normal (approval with diff preview), YOLO mode (--yolo, auto-approves all), AFK mode (--afk, auto-approves and dismisses questions). Status bar badges.
- Sandbox Mode: none — No dedicated sandbox/mode. Has workspace scope boundaries, file operation approval, sensitive file protection (.env, SSH keys). No containerized isolation.
- Resume: full — Full session resume. --session/--resume/-S/-r with optional session ID. Interactive picker. Auto-restores approval decisions, plan mode, subagent instances.
- Continue: full — kimi --continue/-C resumes most recent session in current working directory. /new for fresh session. /sessions lists and switches between sessions.
- Non Interactive: full — --print flag for print mode: auto-exit after prompt, implicit auto-approval (AFK behavior), text stdout. --quiet shorthand. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 75=retryable.
- Output Format: full — --input-format: text or stream-json (JSONL). --output-format: text or stream-json (JSONL). --final-message-only flag. --quiet shorthand. Unified Message format for JSON I/O.
- Statusline: full — Full status bar in shell mode: context usage %, mode indicators, YOLO/AFK badges, plan mode badge, background task counts, provider display name.
- Telemetry: unknown — No explicit telemetry documentation found. Has automatic update checks (disable via KIMI_CLI_NO_AUTO_UPDATE), but no stated telemetry/analytics collection policy.
- Hosted Agent: none — Local CLI tool backed by Moonshot API.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Full custom provider support. providers table in config.toml with type, base_url, api_key, custom_headers. Six provider types including OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini.
- Pricing: Membership (credits) or BYOK
- Notes: Kimi CLI by Moonshot AI (Beijing) is an Apache-2.0 open-source coding agent with rapid development pace (97 releases in 7 months). Strong AGENTS.md, skills, MCP, subagent, and YOLO/AFK approval mode support. Notable for status bar with badges and comprehensive JSON streaming output.

### OpenCode
- Docs: https://opencode.ai/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/sst/opencode
- Website: https://opencode.ai/
- Form factor: CLI, SDK
- Released: 2025
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Supports instruction/config files.
- Skills: full — Supports skills.
- Hooks: full — Config supports commands/hooks workflows.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers.
- Custom Commands: full — Supports commands.
- Subagents: full — Supports custom agents.
- Model Selection: full — Supports provider/model selection.
- Approval Mode: full — Permission controls are configurable.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Permission model controls tool access.
- Resume: full — Supports session options.
- Continue: full — Supports --continue and explicit session selection.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports run mode.
- Output Format: full — CLI supports format options.
- Statusline: unknown
- Telemetry: full — Config includes telemetry-related settings.
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs entirely locally.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Supports many providers and custom provider configuration.
- Pricing: Free (MIT, BYOK), Go $10/mo (first month $5), Zen pay-per-token (min $20), Enterprise per-seat

### Qwen Code
- Docs: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/en/users/overview
- GitHub: https://github.com/QwenLM/qwen-code
- Website: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: Jun '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Hierarchical context files (QWEN.md) at project/user levels. Custom system prompts via --system-prompt and --append-system-prompt. Layered config across system/user/project/env/cli.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills system: SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, model-invoked, personal (~/.qwen/skills/) and project (.qwen/skills/), path gating, extension skills.
- Hooks: full — 14 hook events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd, SubagentStart/Stop, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification, PermissionRequest. Three executors: command, http, function.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP: stdio, SSE, HTTP transports. OAuth 2.0, tool filtering, server trust, global allow/deny lists. CLI management via qwen mcp commands.
- Custom Commands: full — Full custom commands: Markdown files in ~/.qwen/commands/ (global) and .qwen/commands/ (project). Support {{args}}, !{shell}, @{file} references. Namespace support.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent system: named subagents with YAML frontmatter, fork subagents (context-inheriting), per-agent model/permission/tool config, extension subagents.
- Model Selection: full — Full model switching via /model. Supports OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini protocols. Local models via Ollama/vLLM. Per-model reasoning config.
- Approval Mode: full — Four modes: Plan (read-only), Default (manual approval), Auto-Edit (auto file edits, manual shell), YOLO (auto all). Shift+Tab to cycle.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Full sandboxing: macOS Seatbelt (sandbox-exec with 6 profiles) and Docker/Podman containers (full isolation, custom images, network proxying, UID/GID mapping).
- Resume: full — /resume command in interactive mode. --resume <sessionId> in headless mode. Session data as JSONL under ~/.qwen/projects/.
- Continue: full — --continue flag resumes the most recent session for the current project in headless mode.
- Non Interactive: full — -p/--prompt for headless. stdin piping, JSON output, resume/continue, persistent retry mode for CI. GitHub Actions integration.
- Output Format: full — Three formats: text (default), json (structured array), stream-json (line-delimited, real-time). --include-partial-messages for granular streaming.
- Statusline: full — Custom status line via ui.statusLine in settings.json. Shell command receives JSON context. Multi-line support, hot reload, refreshInterval for timer-based updates.
- Telemetry: full — OpenTelemetry-based: traces, logs, metrics. Export to local file, OTLP collector, Alibaba Cloud Managed Service. Privacy opt-out via privacy.usageStatisticsEnabled.
- Hosted Agent: partial — Can use Alibaba Cloud Coding Plan for hosted model access; agent runs locally.
- Custom Model Provider: full — modelProviders config with openai/anthropic/gemini auth types. Local models via any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio). OpenRouter, Fireworks AI.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0)
- Notes: Qwen Code by Alibaba (QwenLM) is the most feature-complete open-source coding agent in this matrix — 20/20 fields at full (tied with Cline). Standout features: macOS Seatbelt + Docker sandbox, OpenTelemetry telemetry, custom status line, three output formats. Apache-2.0, 24.6k stars.

### Roo Code
- Docs: https://docs.roocode.com/
- GitHub: https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code
- Website: https://roocode.com/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension
- Released: Mid-2025
- Latest update: May '26
- Rules: full — Comprehensive: .roorules, .roo/rules/, .clinerules fallback, ~/.roo/rules/ global dir, AGENTS.md/AGENT.md support, mode-specific rules via rules-{modeSlug}.
- Skills: full — Full skills (added v3.38.0, Dec 2025). Follows agentskills.io spec: SKILL.md with frontmatter, progressive discovery, mode-specific targeting, bundled resources.
- Hooks: none — No lifecycle hooks system found in any documentation. Built-in slash commands exist but no user-defined lifecycle hooks.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP since v2.2.0. STDIO, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. Roo Code Marketplace for one-click MCP install. Per-tool alwaysAllow permissions.
- Custom Commands: full — Full slash commands: .roo/commands/ (project) and ~/.roo/commands/ (global). Frontmatter with description, argument-hint, mode targeting. Built-in /init command.
- Subagents: full — Full orchestration via Orchestrator Mode (Boomerang Mode). new_task tool delegates to specialized modes. Isolated context with explicit context transfer.
- Model Selection: full — 22+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, DeepSeek, Google Gemini, LM Studio, Ollama, OpenRouter, and more. Sticky models per mode.
- Approval Mode: full — Granular auto-approval: read/write operations, approved commands (allowlist/denylist), MCP tools (dual permission), mode switching, subtasks, follow-up questions.
- Sandbox Mode: none — No explicit sandbox mode. Checkpoints provide git-based recovery. .rooignore for access control. No isolated/sandboxed execution environment.
- Resume: partial — Checkpoints provide 'Restore Files & Task'. CLI has --create-with-session-id and session resume/history support (v3.51.0+).
- Continue: full — CLI session resume/history support. Per-task history persists and can be resumed. History view shows past tasks for reopening.
- Non Interactive: full — CLI binary (roo) with headless mode. NDJSON stdin/stdout stream protocol. Subcommands: run, list, upgrade. Platform binaries available.
- Output Format: partial — CLI supports NDJSON stdin stream protocol for structured I/O. exitCode in tool_result events. No formal --json or --print flag.
- Statusline: none — No custom status line feature found. VS Code extension has chat panel UI but no configurable status line.
- Telemetry: full — PostHog-powered. Anonymous feature usage, VS Code machine ID, error reports. No PII/code/prompts. Opt-out available. API error telemetry per provider.
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI. Project is now archived.
- Custom Model Provider: full — OpenAI Compatible provider for arbitrary API endpoints. LiteLLM routing. 22+ built-in providers. Debug proxy support. Model-agnostic.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0)
- Notes: Roo Code was a popular Cline fork (24k stars, 574k VS Code installs) that was shut down on May 15, 2026. The repository is now archived and read-only. VS Code extension-host API only; no standalone npm agent SDK. Community forks include ZooCode and Cline.

### Windsurf Cascade
- Docs: https://docs.windsurf.com/
- Website: https://windsurf.com/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension
- Released: Nov '24
- Latest update: Apr '26
- Rules: full — Three scopes: Global (~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/global_rules.md, always-on), Workspace (.windsurf/rules/*.md with frontmatter modes), System (/etc/windsurf/rules/*.md). AGENTS.md support.
- Skills: full — Full support. SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Progressive disclosure. Scopes: Workspace .windsurf/skills/, Global ~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/. Discovers .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/.
- Hooks: full — 12 hook events: pre/post read/write code, pre/post run command, pre/post MCP tool use, pre/post user prompt, post cascade response. Pre-hooks can block with exit code 2.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full native MCP. MCP Marketplace with one-click install. stdio, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. OAuth support. Admin whitelists for teams.
- Custom Commands: full — Custom commands via Workflows (slash commands /[workflow-name]) and Command palette. Terminal has 4 auto-execution levels.
- Subagents: full — Devin Cloud (autonomous agent on own VM), Devin Local Agent, Fast Context (specialized 20x faster retrieval), Agent Command Center manages all agents in Kanban view.
- Model Selection: full — Full selection: SWE-1.6/1.5, Claude Opus/Sonnet, GPT-5.x series, Gemini, DeepSeek V4, Kimi, Grok, and more. Adaptive (intelligent router) and Arena Mode (side-by-side).
- Approval Mode: full — 4 terminal levels: Disabled (require approval), Allowlist Only, Auto (Cascade judges), Turbo (all auto-executed). Allow/deny lists. Cascade Code/Chat/Ask mode distinctions.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Devin cloud agent runs on isolated VM. Git worktrees isolate parallel sessions. Dev Containers support. No standalone sandbox toggle for the IDE.
- Resume: full — Cascade has 'continue' button. Auto-Continue setting. Named checkpoints/snapshots for reversion. Sessions persist and switch via dropdown.
- Continue: full — Auto-Continue setting automatically continues when hitting tool-call limits. Queued messages for follow-ups while Cascade is working. Real-time editor awareness.
- Non Interactive: partial — windsurf CLI available in PATH. Devin CLI agent runs in terminal. No documented --non-interactive flag for the IDE.
- Output Format: partial — Enterprise analytics API available. post_cascade_response_with_transcript hook writes full conversation to JSONL files at ~/.windsurf/transcripts/{trajectory_id}.jsonl.
- Statusline: partial — No customizable status line like Cursor. Status bar has model indicator and plan/usage info. Agent Command Center is a Kanban dashboard of all agents.
- Telemetry: partial — Telemetry settings mentioned in troubleshooting docs. Enterprise analytics API. No documented granular telemetry opt-out beyond standard VS Code settings.
- Hosted Agent: full — Devin cloud agent integration provides managed agent platform.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — BYOK for Anthropic models (Claude Sonnet 4, Opus 4) for Free/Pro users. No generic custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint configuration documented.
- Pricing: Free $0, Pro $20/mo, Max $200/mo, Teams $40/user/mo
- Notes: Windsurf by Codeium (acquired by Cognition AI in April 2026) is a standalone VS Code fork IDE with integrated coding agent (Cascade). Enterprise analytics API only; no agent embed SDK. Competes directly with Cursor IDE.
